figures.caption: Figure 3: Convergence in the L1-norm of the first-order scheme from Section 3.2 and the second-order scheme from Section 3.3.

Figure 5: Temperature and velocity along a horizontal slice for several pressure values 0.1Pa, 4.41Pa, 29.24Pa and 500Pa (horizontal line to line with positive slope). The associated Knudsen numbers are 2×102, 4.6×100, 7.0× 10−1 and 4.1× 10−2.
Figure 6: Heat flux and shear stress for the Fourier and Couette flow as a function of the pressure.
Figure 7: Basic structure and boundary conditions for the orifice flow.
Figure 8: The orifice simulation for outflow pressure po = 50 kPa, and inflow pressures 100 kPa, 75 kPa and 62.5 kPa.
Figure 9: The horizontal velocity of the diatomic ESBGK model for po = 50 kPa and pin = 2po. A vortex is observed behind the orifice.
Figure 10: The orifice simulation for outflow pressure po = 2500Pa, and inflow pressures 5000Pa, 4375Pa, 3750 kPa and 3125Pa.
Figure 12: The pressure (left) and the horizontal velocity (right) for the moving orifice simulation at times 3.11372× 10−6 s (top), 4.10444× 10−6 s (centre) and 4.52904× 10−6 s (bottom).
